summary: Gruber highlights GUIdebook, Marcin Wichary's comprehensive graphical user interface gallery that documents screenshots of UI elements across platforms. He announces an interview he gave to GUIdebook discussing Mac OS X's user interface, covering topics like the first-run experience, the Dock, brushed metal windows, and the Finder. He notes that Wichary even unearthed a screenshot of Apple's proposed purple 'single window mode' button from the original Aqua demo.
key_points: GUIdebook is praised as a uniquely comprehensive and obsessive collection of GUI screenshots and documentation
Gruber participated in an interview covering key Mac OS X interface elements including the Dock, brushed metal, and the Finder
The interview surfaces obscure UI history like Apple's proposed purple 'single window mode' title bar button from the first Aqua demo
money_quotes: GUIdebook is both interesting and amazingly comprehensive, and there's nothing else like it.

Marcin even managed to dig up a screenshot of the purple 'single window mode' button that Apple had proposed putting in the title bar of every single window in the first public demo of the Aqua user interface.
key_insight: GUIdebook represents the kind of obsessive, comprehensive documentation of GUI design history that Gruber values, and the interview surfaces forgotten details from Mac OS X's interface evolution.
